name: CRYPTREC Ciphers List
owner: Digital Agency + Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications + Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry, Japan (CRYPTREC)
description: CRYPTREC LS-0001-2022R2, last updated March 30, 2026. Three tiers: e-Government Recommended Ciphers List (public-key: DSA, ECDSA, EdDSA, RSA-PSS, RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5, RSA-OAEP, DH, ECDH; symmetric: AES, Camellia, KCipher-2; hash: SHA-256/384/512, SHA3 family, SHAKE128/256), Candidate Recommended Ciphers List, and Monitored Ciphers List.
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/cryptrec.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain.

current_extraction.notes: Full text acquired (Japanese; only a Japanese-language PDF exists for the actual ciphers list, despite cryptrec.go.jp/en/ having English-language surrounding pages). PDF was encrypted (decrypted with qpdf), then pdftotext -layout extracted cleanly. IMPORTANT: cipher/algorithm names themselves are written in Latin script even within the Japanese document and are fully readable/extractable (e.g. "AES", "RSA-PSS", "SHA-256") -- genuinely useful data even without translating the surrounding Japanese prose.

scope.notes: Full text acquired (Japanese document; English surrounding context pages also captured).
